_comment_about_this_file: Annotated template for tooling/scaffold-generator/spec/<scaffoldId>.json. Copy this file, rename it to <your-scaffold-id>.json, fill in every field, then run: python tooling/scaffold-generator/generate.py <your-scaffold-id>. The generator validates against governance/schemas/ before writing, so the spec fails fast on type/shape errors. See tooling/scaffold-generator/README.md for full docs and tooling/scaffold-generator/spec/restaurant-hospitality.json for a real worked example.
label: Example Scaffold Label
description: One concise paragraph describing what kind of small-business website this scaffold builds and what the visitor's primary job-to-be-done is. Used in scaffold.json and read by the planner.
_comment_about_routes: Required routes go in defaults (with 'required': true|false). Optional routes use 'optional': true plus a 'when' clause. Order in this array becomes the nav order.

routes.purpose: One-sentence description of what the home page does.
One-sentence description of what the services page does.
One-sentence description of what the about page does.
One-sentence description of what the contact page does.

routes.when: Natural-language condition under which this optional route is added.

_comment_about_sectionsPerRoute: Top-level keys MUST match route ids declared above. Each route lists its required + optional sections and free-form ordering rules the codegen must honour. Section IDs use kebab-case and should be reused across scaffolds where the meaning matches (hero, contact-cta, trust-proof, etc.) so the section-renderer registry stays small.

sectionOrderRules: Hero must be first and include the business type and city.
Trust proof must appear before the final CTA.
Services intro must connect business identity to the offered services.
About story must be specific, not generic 'passion for quality'.
Phone, email and address must all be present.

_comment_about_qualityContract: Scorecard weights are multiplied with per-axis 0-1 scores during eval. mustPass items are deal-breakers: any one failing fails the build. avoid items are negative-list signals for the codegen prompt.

mustPass: Every page has a clear purpose.
Primary CTA is visible above the fold on the homepage.
No lorem ipsum or sample placeholder copy.
All routes from routes.json are present.
Generated code typechecks and the project builds.
avoid: Random color choices not derived from variant tokens.
Forms or features that fail at build because of missing env.

_comment_about_compatibleDossiers: Use REAL Dossier IDs from packages/generation/orchestration/dossiers/{soft,hard}/. Sections (hero, service-list, contact-cta) do NOT belong here — they go in sectionsPerRoute. 'required' Dossiers always mount; 'recommended' mount when brief signals support them; 'conditional' need a 'when' clause; 'disallowedByDefault' are hard-blocked even if planner finds them.

compatibleDossiers.comment: Optional free-form note that ends up as _comment in the generated file.

conditional.when: the brand explicitly wants a playable game element

_comment_about_selectionProfile: Used by the planner's classifier. embeddingText is the long paragraph fed to the embedding model. semanticSignals/negativeSignals are kebab-case keyword hints. llmClassificationHints are natural-language sentences the small-LLM reranker uses. minConfidence and requiresTieBreakWhenWithin tune the dispatcher's strictness.

selectionProfile.embeddingText: One paragraph (3-5 sentences) describing what kind of business this scaffold fits, written for an embedding model. Mention domain words, business types, visitor goals.
llmClassificationHints: Do not choose this scaffold when ...

_comment_about_variants: 3-5 variants per scaffold is the sweet spot. Each variant is a complete visual expression (color, type, radius, spacing, motion). NO routes/sections/dossiers per variant — variants are purely stylistic. scaleRatio must be in [1.0, 1.6].

variants.label: Example Variant One
variants.description: Short paragraph describing the visual feel: palette inspiration, mood, target sub-segment.
